title: woof gate front-matter
description: YAML front-matter of .woof/epics/E<N>/gate.md. Validates the structured header only; prose sections are unconstrained markdown. The gate.md represents an OPEN gate; resolution is recorded as a jsonl event with a decision drawn from the taxonomy in jsonl-events.schema.json (gate_resolved.decision). triggered_by[] enum values are registry-derived check IDs plus graph-level triggers; the enum is snapshot-tested against the registry in tests/unit/test_check_stage_5_subcommand.py.

type.description: plan_gate (Stage 4, always opens). story_gate (Stage 5 halt, conditional on check failure or executor halt). review_gate (periodic; opens every-N stories or before epic close to surface accumulated minor critique findings). readiness_gate (Stage 2.5; opens when the deterministic contract-readiness node finds EPIC.md not concrete enough for planning).

stage.description: Stage that wrote the gate. Target shape: readiness_gate=2 (the post-definition Stage-2.5 readiness boundary; integers cannot express 2.5, so the gate carries the stage of the contract it validates), plan_gate=4, story_gate=5, review_gate=5. Legacy stage=6 artefacts remain read-tolerated for migration.

story_id.description: Story this gate is for. Required (non-null) when type=story_gate; null for plan_gate. For review_gate, may be null (end-of-epic) or the boundary story (every-N rollup).

triggered_by.description: Reasons the gate opened. plan_review fires automatically at Stage 4. readiness_unready opens the Stage-2.5 readiness gate when the deterministic contract-readiness node finds EPIC.md not ready for planning. readiness_escalation is the same gate type but signals that the configured escalation threshold of failed readiness cycles has been reached; it resolves through the same verbs as readiness_unready (approve_with_reason, revise_epic_contract, abandon_epic). check_1_quality_gates..check_9_review_valve map to Stage-5 registry check IDs (canonical names; these replace the legacy check_1_quality_gate etc.). empty_diff_review opens when a story finishes with no diff. Graph-level: subprocess_crash (producer process exited non-zero), subprocess_aborted (SIGTERM/timeout), executor_aborted (producer wrote outcome=aborted_with_position), reviewer_unreachable (reviewer route exited before producing critique), incomplete_stage_state (required Stage-5 handoff artefact is missing or malformed). schema_validation_failed when Stage 3 self-validation exhausts the 2-attempt cap. tracker_sync_conflict when an issue-tracker push detects remote changes (github_sync_conflict retained as a legacy alias). manual for human-opened gates.

exit_code.description: Subprocess exit code. Required when triggered_by contains subprocess_crash.

harness_session_id.description: CC session UUID or Codex audit-file path of the subprocess that caused the gate.
